title: Bond mints redeemable index tokens before collecting the full basket, enabling malicious-asset reentrancy
reason: Reentrancy is theoretically possible, but no concrete path was shown to extract net value or leave the index undercollateralized: if the outer `bond()` later fails the whole transaction reverts, and if it succeeds the full basket is still collected.

title: Unrestricted Rescue Functions Allow Draining of Protocol Assets
reason: `rescueERC20()` and `rescueETH()` send funds to `Ownable(address(V3_TWAP_UTILS)).owner()`, not to the caller. Anyone can trigger an admin rescue, but that is not an attacker-controlled drain.

title: Unrestricted Staking Pool Token Transfers Bypass Stake Restrictions
reason: The restriction only limits who may be minted staking receipts in `stake()`. Transferring receipt tokens later does not mint extra stake or create a realistic protocol-level loss scenario.

title: Division by Zero in WeightedIndex Constructor
reason: This is a deployment-time misconfiguration only, not an exploitable vulnerability in a live protocol.

title: Uniswap V2 Price Manipulation via Flash Loans
reason: The reserve-derived price helpers are only exposed through view functions; `bond()` and `debond()` do not use these prices for settlement.

title: Flash Loan Callback Without Reentrancy Protection
reason: No concrete unsafe state dependency was identified. The callback must restore the borrowed token balance before returning, and the report did not show a profitable reentrant path.

title: Insufficient Slippage Protection on Token Swap
reason: This is ordinary slippage/MEV exposure around an allowed swap, not a distinct code bug with a demonstrated protocol-breaking path.

title: No Validation of Token Decimals in WeightedIndex
reason: This depends on deploying an index with malicious or highly non-standard token metadata and does not present a realistic permissionless exploit against an already deployed index.

title: Unchecked Return Value in ETH Transfer
reason: The contract does check the result of the ETH send and reverts on failure via `require(_sent, 'SENT')`.

title: Potential Integer Overflow in Price Calculations
reason: The cited math is in view-only pricing helpers, not in settlement logic, and no realistic overflow exploit path was demonstrated for standard reserve and decimal ranges.

title: Missing Zero-Address Validation in Constructor
reason: This is a deployment/configuration risk rather than a live, permissionless vulnerability.

title: Missing Access Control on StakingPoolToken Admin Functions
reason: If restrictions are disabled at deployment, the inability to later enable them is a governance/design limitation, not a realistic protocol-harm vulnerability.
